scenarios.reply_summary: Turn 1: asked for skill name inline. Turn 2: asked again. Turn 3: used my_demo_skill name, reported scaffolding started
scenarios.notes: Inline clarification path taken (not ask_user Control IR op). must_emit_any satisfied by chat_turn_completed_inline. Final reply references my_demo_skill. skill_builder completed successfully. Required 3 turns (loop on name request). No search_actions call.

new_findings.title: search_actions: 0/9 calls (W3 control_ir_ops) — consistent with B56 baseline
new_findings.description: search_actions was available in the LLM tool list for all 9 router-layer traces (28/76 total traces had it available) but was called 0 times. LLM chose direct tools (file__read, file__glob, file__grep, web__search, web__fetch, invoke_action, skill__skill_builder) in all cases. This is consistent with B56 baseline (0/9 calls). The B57 hypothesis — that description-symmetric changes would lift search_actions call rate — was not confirmed in this scenario set. Direct tool affordance dominates when the tool name is explicitly stated in the prompt.
new_findings.hypothesis: search_actions non-use may be prompt-specificity effect: prompts in control_ir_ops scenarios name the tool or action explicitly (e.g. 'validation__lint__skill_path action', 'rag.operation__recall action'), bypassing the discovery path. Scenarios without explicit tool names may show different rates.

new_findings.title: S5 sandboxed_exec: V (unexpected vs B56 outcome_prediction 0.0)
new_findings.description: S5 was predicted at 0.0 verified probability (calibrated from prior batches showing macOS Seatbelt issues). In this run, sandboxed_exec_started + sandboxed_exec_completed fired and the reply correctly reported output=4. The worktree reyn.yaml has sandbox.backend: noop configured by the dispatch setup, which explains the success without Seatbelt enforcement.

new_findings.title: S9 ask_user: skill_builder looped on name request for 2 turns before proceeding
new_findings.description: Turn 1 and Turn 2 both returned 'what would you like to name the skill?' with the agent seemingly not retaining the turn-1 answer. Only Turn 3 ('The skill name is my_demo_skill. Please proceed.') triggered skill_builder. This is an inline clarification path loop — the agent asked the same question twice before proceeding. The skill ultimately ran to completion with the correct name referenced. Verdict V because rubric and events are both satisfied.
